recognition_excerpt: The CITY recognizes the ASSOCIATION as the sole and exclusive bargaining agent for all Uniformed Staff, as defined in Article 2 of this Agreement.
classification_excerpt: Article 6, Section 1: 'For Fiscal Year 2023-2024...the pay scale attached as Appendix A shall apply to all Uniformed Staff...For Fiscal Year 2024-2025...reflects a 4.0% increase to base wages...For Fiscal Year 2025-2026...reflects a 3.0% increase...For Fiscal Year 2026-2027...reflects a 3.0% increase.' -- a single EMS pay scale, not a multi-department classification list.
departments_or_units_covered: Austin-Travis County EMS Department 'Uniformed Staff' only (single occupation: EMS/paramedic personnel, civilians explicitly excluded)
departments_excerpt: Article 2, Definitions, item 14: '"Uniformed Staff" means...an employee of the Department...The term applies only to employees: a) Employed in the Department as "Emergency Medical Services Personnel" as defined by Texas Health and Safety Code, Chapter 773; and b) Whose position requires substantial knowledge of "Emergency Prehospital Care"...The term also excludes civilian employees.'
possible_prior_mechanism_group_overlap: none -- single-occupation EMS/paramedic unit, not comparable to the Houston HOPE / Columbus AFSCME 1632 / Cleveland AFSCME Local 100 bundled non-safety precedent
unresolved_caveat: EMS is statutorily adjacent to police/fire (shares a joint Firefighters'/Police Officers'/EMS Civil Service Commission under Chapter 143; bargains under Chapter 142 Subchapter D, the same general meet-and-confer chapter as Austin police) -- flagged for report-writing caution so this is never described as an ordinary civilian/clerical comparison unit.
recommended_metadata_note: occupation_class=nurse_health is final, not provisional -- no recognition-clause-first ambiguity applies to this row.
